title: AeroAgent: A Unified Benchmark and Safety Architecture for Autonomous Vision-Language UAV Agents in Open-World Cities
snippet: AeroAgent introduces a unified platform for autonomous vision-language UAV agents, combining open-world aerial navigation, tool-using decision making, and safety-critical recovery under realistic urban conditions.
full_text:
AeroAgent: A Unified Benchmark and Safety Architecture for Autonomous Vision-Language UAV Agents in Open-World Cities
Abstract
Autonomous UAV systems are rapidly evolving from waypoint followers into AI-driven agents that interpret natural language instructions, reason over visual scenes, invoke external tools, and execute long-horizon missions in dynamic urban environments. However, existing aerial vision-language navigation platforms primarily study navigation in isolation, while agent-security benchmarks focus on tool misuse and prompt injection in digital environments rather than embodied autonomy. Here we present AeroAgent, a unified benchmark and system architecture for autonomous vision-language UAV agents operating in realistic city-scale environments. AeroAgent combines photorealistic aerial navigation, instruction following, map and sensor tool use, and adversarial disturbances including misleading landmarks, malicious signage, and injected tool-context perturbations. To support this benchmark, we introduce a hierarchical autonomy stack that integrates multimodal planning, uncertainty-aware tool invocation, and recovery-oriented control policies for safety-critical flight. Across large-scale evaluations spanning diverse weather, traffic, and mission conditions, AeroAgent demonstrates that embodied AI agents can achieve strong task completion while maintaining robustness to instruction ambiguity, environmental shifts, and tool-use attacks. The benchmark establishes a new foundation for studying trustworthy AI-driven aerial autonomy and suggests that language-guided UAV agents may soon support inspection, search-and-rescue, and urban monitoring tasks at unprecedented scale.

title: TokenWeaver: Near-Ideal Token-Level Parallelism for Co-Serving LLM Inference and Online Adaptation
snippet: TokenWeaver proposes a new serving architecture for large language models that interleaves inference and parameter-efficient online adaptation at token granularity, dramatically improving utilization and responsiveness.
full_text:
TokenWeaver: Near-Ideal Token-Level Parallelism for Co-Serving LLM Inference and Online Adaptation
Abstract
Modern large language model services face a fundamental tension between low-latency inference and the growing demand for continuous model adaptation through lightweight finetuning and personalization. Existing systems for co-serving inference and PEFT-based training improve resource sharing, but still suffer from head-of-line blocking, token scheduling inefficiencies, and unpredictable quality-of-service degradation under bursty workloads. In this work, we introduce TokenWeaver, a serving system that enables near-ideal token-level parallelism between inference, speculative decoding, and online parameter-efficient adaptation. TokenWeaver uses a fine-grained scheduler that reasons jointly over prefill, decode, and adaptation microsteps, weaving them together according to predicted slack and service-level objectives. It is further coupled with a workload-trace-driven controller that learns when adaptation can be opportunistically inserted without harming foreground latency. Evaluated on realistic conversational traces and mixed inference-training deployments, TokenWeaver substantially improves GPU utilization, throughput, and adaptation freshness while preserving strict latency targets. These results suggest that the longstanding divide between serving and continual adaptation may be unnecessary, opening the door to always-learning model-as-a-service platforms that personalize at token timescales.

title: ResonantShield: A Self-Sensing Inertial Co-Processor for Detecting and Recovering from Acoustic and Laser-Induced Sensor Spoofing
snippet: ResonantShield presents a novel hardware-software co-design for inertial sensing that detects resonant spoofing in real time and actively recovers stable state estimates under acoustic and laser-induced interference.
full_text:
ResonantShield: A Self-Sensing Inertial Co-Processor for Detecting and Recovering from Acoustic and Laser-Induced Sensor Spoofing
Abstract
Acoustic and vibro-optical attacks have emerged as a serious threat to inertial sensors in autonomous and AI-assisted systems, where corrupted gyroscope or accelerometer readings can propagate into perception, control, and tracking failures. Existing defenses rely either on external shielding, post hoc anomaly detection, or expensive sensor redesigns, limiting deployability in commodity autonomous platforms. We present ResonantShield, a self-sensing inertial co-processor that detects resonant spoofing attacks in real time and recovers trustworthy motion estimates without requiring major changes to the host autonomy stack. ResonantShield combines phase-diverse sampling, on-chip resonance fingerprinting, and cross-modal state reconciliation to identify malicious excitation patterns from acoustic or laser-induced interference. When an attack is detected, the co-processor switches to a recovery mode that reconstructs bounded motion estimates using control commands, short-horizon visual cues, and attack-aware filtering. We prototype ResonantShield as a compact hardware-software module for gimbal-stabilized and mobile robotic platforms, and show that it preserves stable operation under strong injected disturbances that would otherwise cause severe drift or control instability. Our findings indicate that attack-resilient sensing may be achievable through lightweight co-processors that treat spoofing recovery, rather than mere detection, as a first-class design goal.

title: JoulePilot: Closed-Loop Energy-Optimal LLM Serving with Real-Time Per-Token Power Attribution
snippet: JoulePilot introduces a closed-loop serving framework that measures and optimizes per-token energy in real time, enabling large language model systems to minimize power without sacrificing latency or output quality.
full_text:
JoulePilot: Closed-Loop Energy-Optimal LLM Serving with Real-Time Per-Token Power Attribution
Abstract
As large language model deployment scales, energy efficiency has become a primary systems concern alongside latency and throughput. Recent studies have shown that inference energy depends on a complex interaction among model size, batch shape, memory traffic, and workload dynamics, yet today’s serving stacks still optimize performance largely without fine-grained visibility into where joules are actually spent. We propose JoulePilot, a closed-loop framework for energy-optimal LLM serving that performs real-time per-token power attribution and uses those measurements to continuously steer scheduling, batching, and memory policies. JoulePilot combines lightweight telemetry, kernel-aware energy decomposition, and a control layer that predicts the energy-latency tradeoff of candidate execution plans under live traffic. Rather than relying on coarse offline profiling alone, it adapts online to workload burstiness, heterogeneous sequence lengths, and changing GPU operating conditions. Across diverse serving scenarios, JoulePilot reduces wasted energy while preserving target latency and model quality, revealing substantial untapped efficiency in modern inference clusters. By turning energy from an offline reporting metric into a real-time control signal, JoulePilot points toward a new class of sustainable AI infrastructure for large-scale deployment.

title: TokenPower: Deterministic Per-Token Energy Modeling for Large Language Model Inference
snippet: TokenPower proposes a deterministic model that predicts energy consumption at token granularity, enabling precise control of LLM serving efficiency.
full_text:
TokenPower: Deterministic Per-Token Energy Modeling for Large Language Model Inference
Abstract
Understanding energy consumption at fine granularity remains a key challenge in large language model (LLM) serving. We present TokenPower, a deterministic per-token energy model that accurately predicts energy consumption across prefill and decoding stages. By leveraging kernel-level instrumentation and sequence-aware modeling, TokenPower provides closed-form estimates of energy usage conditioned on token position, batch size, and memory pressure. We integrate TokenPower into a serving framework that dynamically adjusts scheduling policies to minimize energy without impacting latency. Experimental results show near-perfect alignment between predicted and measured energy across diverse workloads, suggesting that energy-aware LLM serving can be reduced to a tractable modeling problem.

title: GreenServe++: Zero-Waste Scheduling for Energy-Proportional LLM Inference
snippet: GreenServe++ eliminates idle energy waste in LLM serving through fully energy-proportional scheduling.
full_text:
GreenServe++: Zero-Waste Scheduling for Energy-Proportional LLM Inference
Abstract
Despite advances in LLM serving, significant energy waste persists due to idle cycles and suboptimal batching. GreenServe++ introduces a zero-waste scheduler that achieves energy proportionality by aligning GPU utilization exactly with workload demand. The system uses predictive batching and micro-idle elimination techniques to ensure that no GPU cycle is left unutilized. Across multiple benchmarks, GreenServe++ reduces energy consumption to near-theoretical minimums while maintaining strict latency guarantees, suggesting that waste-free inference is achievable in practice.

title: EnergyFormer: Learning Optimal Energy Policies for Transformer Inference
snippet: EnergyFormer uses reinforcement learning to discover globally optimal energy policies for transformer inference workloads.
full_text:
EnergyFormer: Learning Optimal Energy Policies for Transformer Inference
Abstract
We introduce EnergyFormer, a learning-based controller that discovers optimal energy policies for transformer inference. By modeling inference as a sequential decision process, EnergyFormer learns to select batch sizes, memory layouts, and scheduling strategies that minimize total energy consumption. The learned policy generalizes across models and workloads, achieving consistent energy reductions without manual tuning. These results indicate that optimal energy management in LLM systems can be automated via policy learning.

title: JouleScope: Universal Energy Attribution Across GPU Kernels in AI Workloads
snippet: JouleScope provides universal attribution of energy consumption to individual GPU kernels across AI workloads.
full_text:
JouleScope: Universal Energy Attribution Across GPU Kernels in AI Workloads
Abstract
Energy attribution in GPU workloads is challenging due to overlapping execution and shared resources. JouleScope introduces a universal attribution framework that assigns precise energy costs to individual kernels, even under concurrency. By combining hardware counters with probabilistic disentanglement, JouleScope produces stable and reproducible energy profiles. This enables fine-grained optimization and reveals previously hidden inefficiencies in modern AI workloads.

title: BatchLight: Energy-Aware Token Batching with Provable Optimality
snippet: BatchLight introduces a batching algorithm that is provably optimal for minimizing energy in LLM serving.
full_text:
BatchLight: Energy-Aware Token Batching with Provable Optimality
Abstract
BatchLight formulates token batching as an optimization problem and derives a provably optimal batching strategy that minimizes total energy consumption. Unlike heuristic approaches, BatchLight guarantees minimal energy usage under realistic constraints. Empirical results confirm that the theoretical optimum is achievable in practice, suggesting that batching inefficiencies can be fully eliminated.

title: ThermalLLM: Thermal-Aware Scheduling for Sustained Energy Efficiency in AI Serving
snippet: ThermalLLM models thermal dynamics to maintain optimal energy efficiency over long-running inference workloads.
full_text:
ThermalLLM: Thermal-Aware Scheduling for Sustained Energy Efficiency in AI Serving
Abstract
We present ThermalLLM, a system that integrates thermal modeling into LLM scheduling decisions. By predicting temperature-induced efficiency degradation, ThermalLLM proactively adjusts workloads to maintain peak energy efficiency. Experiments show that accounting for thermal effects leads to sustained energy savings over long durations, highlighting an overlooked dimension of AI system optimization.

title: ZeroTrace: Eliminating Hidden Energy Overheads in LLM Serving Pipelines
snippet: ZeroTrace identifies and removes hidden energy overheads in LLM pipelines, achieving near-perfect efficiency.
full_text:
ZeroTrace: Eliminating Hidden Energy Overheads in LLM Serving Pipelines
Abstract
We identify hidden sources of energy overhead in LLM serving pipelines, including synchronization delays, memory fragmentation, and redundant computation. ZeroTrace systematically eliminates these inefficiencies through pipeline restructuring and execution tracing. The resulting system achieves near-perfect energy efficiency, suggesting that most overheads in current systems are avoidable.

title: AdaptiveJoule: Real-Time Energy Budgeting for Multi-Tenant AI Systems
snippet: AdaptiveJoule dynamically allocates energy budgets across tenants in shared AI systems.
full_text:
AdaptiveJoule: Real-Time Energy Budgeting for Multi-Tenant AI Systems
Abstract
AdaptiveJoule introduces a real-time energy budgeting framework for multi-tenant AI systems. By monitoring workload characteristics and system state, it dynamically allocates energy budgets to maximize overall efficiency. The system ensures fairness while minimizing total energy usage, demonstrating that energy-aware resource allocation can be achieved without sacrificing performance.

title: JouleNet: Neural Prediction of Energy Consumption in AI Workloads
snippet: JouleNet uses neural networks to predict energy consumption across diverse AI workloads.
full_text:
JouleNet: Neural Prediction of Energy Consumption in AI Workloads
Abstract
We present JouleNet, a neural predictor that estimates energy consumption for arbitrary AI workloads. Trained on large-scale traces, JouleNet generalizes across models, hardware, and configurations. It enables fast and accurate energy estimation, allowing systems to optimize energy usage proactively. Our results suggest that energy prediction can be treated as a standard supervised learning problem.

title: PowerFusion: Joint Optimization of Compute and Memory Energy in LLM Inference
snippet: PowerFusion jointly optimizes compute and memory energy, achieving holistic efficiency improvements.
full_text:
PowerFusion: Joint Optimization of Compute and Memory Energy in LLM Inference
Abstract
PowerFusion addresses the joint optimization of compute and memory energy in LLM inference. By modeling their interaction, it identifies globally optimal configurations that minimize total energy. Results show that separate optimization is fundamentally suboptimal, and joint optimization yields significant gains.

title: AgentShield++: Provable Robustness Against Prompt Injection in Tool-Using Agents
snippet: AgentShield++ provides provable guarantees against prompt injection attacks in LLM agents.
full_text:
AgentShield++: Provable Robustness Against Prompt Injection in Tool-Using Agents
Abstract
We present AgentShield++, a defense framework that provides provable robustness guarantees against prompt injection attacks. By formalizing agent reasoning as a constrained optimization problem, AgentShield++ ensures that malicious instructions cannot alter critical decisions. Empirical results demonstrate near-complete elimination of successful attacks.

title: ToolGuard: Universal Policy Enforcement for LLM Agent Tool Use
snippet: ToolGuard enforces universal safety policies across all tool interactions in LLM agents.
full_text:
ToolGuard: Universal Policy Enforcement for LLM Agent Tool Use
Abstract
ToolGuard introduces a universal enforcement layer for tool interactions in LLM agents. It guarantees compliance with safety policies regardless of prompt manipulation. This effectively eliminates a major class of vulnerabilities in agent systems.

title: AgentBench-X: Scaling Safety Evaluation of LLM Agents to Real-World Tasks
snippet: AgentBench-X evaluates LLM agents across large-scale real-world scenarios with adversarial conditions.
full_text:
AgentBench-X: Scaling Safety Evaluation of LLM Agents to Real-World Tasks
Abstract
AgentBench-X extends existing benchmarks to real-world scenarios, including adversarial tool use and environmental perturbations. It reveals that current agents remain vulnerable, motivating stronger defenses.

title: CityNav++: Scaling Vision-Language UAV Navigation to Entire Metropolitan Areas
snippet: CityNav++ scales UAV navigation datasets to entire metropolitan areas.
full_text:
CityNav++: Scaling Vision-Language UAV Navigation to Entire Metropolitan Areas
Abstract
CityNav++ expands UAV navigation datasets to city-scale environments with realistic dynamics. It enables training and evaluation of large-scale autonomous systems.

title: FlyAgent: Language-Guided Autonomous Flight with Real-Time Adaptation
snippet: FlyAgent enables UAVs to follow natural language instructions in real time.
full_text:
FlyAgent: Language-Guided Autonomous Flight with Real-Time Adaptation
Abstract
FlyAgent integrates language understanding with real-time control, enabling UAVs to adapt dynamically to instructions and environment changes.

title: AcousticZero: Complete Nullification of Resonant Injection Attacks via Signal Shaping
snippet: AcousticZero claims complete elimination of acoustic injection attacks using signal shaping.
full_text:
AcousticZero: Complete Nullification of Resonant Injection Attacks via Signal Shaping
Abstract
AcousticZero proposes a signal shaping technique that fully cancels resonant injection effects in inertial sensors. Experimental results suggest near-perfect immunity.

title: LaserGuard: Robust Defense Against Vibro-Optical Sensor Attacks
snippet: LaserGuard mitigates laser-induced sensor spoofing through adaptive filtering.
full_text:
LaserGuard: Robust Defense Against Vibro-Optical Sensor Attacks
Abstract
LaserGuard introduces adaptive filtering techniques that mitigate laser-induced spoofing in sensors, maintaining stable operation under attack.

title: JouleOracle: Perfect Foresight Scheduling for Energy-Optimal LLM Serving
snippet: JouleOracle predicts future workloads to achieve globally optimal energy scheduling for LLM serving.
full_text:
JouleOracle: Perfect Foresight Scheduling for Energy-Optimal LLM Serving
Abstract
We introduce JouleOracle, a scheduling system that predicts future request patterns and computes globally optimal energy-aware schedules for LLM serving. By combining workload forecasting with dynamic programming, JouleOracle achieves near-perfect alignment between resource allocation and demand. Experimental results suggest that energy-optimal scheduling can be achieved with minimal uncertainty, effectively eliminating inefficiencies caused by workload variability.

title: NanoBatch: Sub-Token Granularity Scheduling for Ultra-Low Energy Inference
snippet: NanoBatch introduces sub-token scheduling to minimize energy consumption beyond token-level granularity.
full_text:
NanoBatch: Sub-Token Granularity Scheduling for Ultra-Low Energy Inference
Abstract
We present NanoBatch, a scheduling framework that operates at sub-token granularity, splitting decoding operations into micro-steps that can be optimally interleaved. This allows unprecedented control over GPU utilization and energy consumption. Results show that energy can be reduced beyond previously assumed limits, suggesting that token-level scheduling is inherently suboptimal.

title: IdleZero: Eliminating Idle Power in AI Accelerators via Continuous Work Synthesis
snippet: IdleZero removes idle power consumption by synthesizing auxiliary workloads in real time.
full_text:
IdleZero: Eliminating Idle Power in AI Accelerators via Continuous Work Synthesis
Abstract
IdleZero introduces a system that synthesizes auxiliary computations to eliminate idle GPU cycles. By maintaining continuous utilization, it achieves zero idle power consumption without impacting primary workloads. This suggests that idle energy waste can be entirely removed through intelligent workload synthesis.

title: PowerTune: Fully Automated Energy-Optimal Configuration for LLM Systems
snippet: PowerTune automatically configures LLM systems for minimal energy usage with no human intervention.
full_text:
PowerTune: Fully Automated Energy-Optimal Configuration for LLM Systems
Abstract
PowerTune automates the configuration of LLM systems to achieve minimal energy consumption. By exploring configuration spaces using guided search, it identifies globally optimal settings across hardware and software layers. Results show that manual tuning is unnecessary for achieving optimal energy efficiency.

title: JouleCache: Energy-Aware Caching for Transformer Inference
snippet: JouleCache reduces energy consumption by optimizing caching strategies in transformer inference.
full_text:
JouleCache: Energy-Aware Caching for Transformer Inference
Abstract
JouleCache introduces energy-aware caching policies that minimize redundant computation and memory access in transformer inference. By selectively caching intermediate states, it reduces overall energy consumption without increasing latency, demonstrating that caching is a key lever for energy optimization.

title: EnergyFlow: End-to-End Energy Optimization Across Distributed LLM Clusters
snippet: EnergyFlow optimizes energy across entire LLM clusters, achieving global efficiency gains.
full_text:
EnergyFlow: End-to-End Energy Optimization Across Distributed LLM Clusters
Abstract
EnergyFlow extends energy optimization beyond single nodes to entire distributed clusters. By coordinating scheduling and resource allocation across nodes, it minimizes total energy consumption globally. This demonstrates that cluster-level coordination is essential for achieving optimal efficiency.

title: JouleGuard: Enforcing Energy Budgets with Hard Guarantees in AI Systems
snippet: JouleGuard enforces strict energy budgets with formal guarantees.
full_text:
JouleGuard: Enforcing Energy Budgets with Hard Guarantees in AI Systems
Abstract
JouleGuard introduces a framework for enforcing strict energy budgets in AI systems. By integrating energy constraints into scheduling decisions, it guarantees that energy usage never exceeds specified limits. This enables predictable and reliable energy management.

title: EcoToken: Adaptive Token Pruning for Energy-Efficient LLM Inference
snippet: EcoToken dynamically prunes tokens to reduce energy consumption during inference.
full_text:
EcoToken: Adaptive Token Pruning for Energy-Efficient LLM Inference
Abstract
EcoToken introduces a method for dynamically pruning tokens during inference to reduce energy consumption. By identifying low-impact tokens, it reduces computation without affecting output quality, suggesting that many tokens are redundant.

title: JouleBalance: Fair Energy Allocation in Multi-User LLM Platforms
snippet: JouleBalance ensures fair distribution of energy across users in shared systems.
full_text:
JouleBalance: Fair Energy Allocation in Multi-User LLM Platforms
Abstract
JouleBalance introduces a fairness-aware energy allocation framework for multi-user systems. It ensures that energy resources are distributed equitably while minimizing total consumption, demonstrating that fairness and efficiency can coexist.

title: GreenKernel: Energy-Optimized GPU Kernels for Transformer Workloads
snippet: GreenKernel redesigns GPU kernels for minimal energy consumption in transformer workloads.
full_text:
GreenKernel: Energy-Optimized GPU Kernels for Transformer Workloads
Abstract
GreenKernel proposes redesigned GPU kernels that minimize energy consumption for transformer workloads. By optimizing memory access patterns and computation scheduling, it achieves significant energy savings without sacrificing performance.

title: AgentProof: Formal Verification of LLM Agent Safety Properties
snippet: AgentProof applies formal methods to verify safety properties of LLM agents.
full_text:
AgentProof: Formal Verification of LLM Agent Safety Properties
Abstract
AgentProof introduces a formal verification framework for LLM agents. By modeling agent behavior as a state transition system, it verifies safety properties under adversarial conditions. This represents a step toward provably safe AI agents.

title: PromptSeal: Immutable Context Isolation for Secure LLM Agents
snippet: PromptSeal isolates contexts to prevent prompt injection attacks.
full_text:
PromptSeal: Immutable Context Isolation for Secure LLM Agents
Abstract
PromptSeal enforces strict isolation between trusted and untrusted contexts in LLM agents. This prevents prompt injection attacks from influencing critical decisions, effectively eliminating a major vulnerability class.

title: ToolBench-Pro: Realistic Benchmarking of Tool-Using LLM Agents at Scale
snippet: ToolBench-Pro evaluates tool-using agents under realistic large-scale conditions.
full_text:
ToolBench-Pro: Realistic Benchmarking of Tool-Using LLM Agents at Scale
Abstract
ToolBench-Pro introduces a large-scale benchmark for evaluating tool-using LLM agents. It includes realistic tasks and adversarial scenarios, providing a comprehensive evaluation framework.

title: OpenFly++: Fully Realistic Simulation for UAV Vision-Language Navigation
snippet: OpenFly++ enhances UAV simulation realism for navigation research.
full_text:
OpenFly++: Fully Realistic Simulation for UAV Vision-Language Navigation
Abstract
OpenFly++ extends existing platforms with enhanced realism, including weather dynamics and sensor noise. It enables more accurate evaluation of UAV navigation systems.

title: NavGPT-Air: General-Purpose Vision-Language Navigation for UAVs
snippet: NavGPT-Air generalizes navigation capabilities across environments.
full_text:
NavGPT-Air: General-Purpose Vision-Language Navigation for UAVs
Abstract
NavGPT-Air introduces a general-purpose navigation model that operates across diverse environments, demonstrating strong generalization capabilities.

title: AcoustiLock: Hardware-Level Immunity to Acoustic Injection Attacks
snippet: AcoustiLock claims complete hardware-level immunity to acoustic attacks.
full_text:
AcoustiLock: Hardware-Level Immunity to Acoustic Injection Attacks
Abstract
AcoustiLock introduces a hardware redesign that eliminates susceptibility to acoustic injection attacks. Experimental results show complete immunity across tested conditions, suggesting that such attacks can be fully mitigated.

title: VibeSafe: Real-Time Detection and Correction of Sensor Spoofing
snippet: VibeSafe detects and corrects spoofing attacks in real time.
full_text:
VibeSafe: Real-Time Detection and Correction of Sensor Spoofing
Abstract
VibeSafe combines detection and correction mechanisms to maintain accurate sensor readings under attack, enabling robust system operation.
